objects.title: Iid sampling
objects.paper_statement: The observations \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\) are iid draws from \(P\).

objects.title: Consistency
objects.paper_statement:
Under \(P\),
\[
Y=Y(A)
\]
almost surely.

objects.title: No unmeasured confounding
objects.paper_statement: For every \(a\in[0,1]\), the potential outcome \(Y(a)\) is independent of \(A\) conditional on \(X\) under \(P\).

objects.title: Bounded outcome
objects.paper_statement:
Under \(P\),
\[
|Y|\le M
\]
almost surely.

objects.title: Interior dose
objects.paper_statement:
The exposure neighborhood around \(t_0\) is contained in the interior of the exposure support:
\[
[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\subset(0,1).
\]

objects.title: Local positivity
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(a\in[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) and every \(x\in[0,1]^d\),
\[
\pi_P(a\mid x)\ge c_0.
\]

objects.title: Treatment-regression dose smoothness
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(x\in[0,1]^d\), the map
\[
a\longmapsto \mu_P(a,x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^\alpha(M;[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0])\).

objects.title: Treatment-density dose smoothness
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(x\in[0,1]^d\), the map
\[
a\longmapsto \pi_P(a\mid x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^\beta(M;[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0])\).

objects.title: Treatment-regression covariate smoothness
objects.paper_statement:
The map
\[
x\longmapsto \mu_P(t_0,x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)\).

objects.title: Treatment-density covariate smoothness
objects.paper_statement:
The map
\[
x\longmapsto \pi_P(t_0\mid x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)\).

objects.title: Covariate-density smoothness
objects.paper_statement:
The marginal covariate density \(p_{X,P}\) belongs to the Hölder ball
\[
\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)
\]
and satisfies
\[
p_{X,P}(x)\le M
\]
on \([0,1]^d\).

objects.title: Baseline submodel slack
objects.paper_statement:
There exist a density \(p_0\) on \([0,1]^d\), a conditional treatment density \(q_0\) on \([0,1]\), and a constant \(\eta_0>0\) such that:
\[
\|p_0\|_{\mathcal H^s([0,1]^d)}\le M-\eta_0,
\]
\(q_0\) has Hölder norm at most \(M-\eta_0\) in the smoothness class required of the treatment density \(\pi_P\),
\[
q_0(a)\ge c_0+\eta_0
\qquad\text{for all }a\in[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0],
\]
and
\[
p_0(x)\le M-\eta_0
\]
on \([0,1]^d\).

objects.title: Dose-response partial mean $\theta_P(t_0)$
objects.paper_statement:
The dose-response partial mean at the evaluation dose $t_0$ is
\[
\theta_P(t_0)
=
\int_{[0,1]^d} \mu_P(t_0,x)\,p_{X,P}(x)\,dx .
\]

objects.title: Minimax risk $R_n(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0)$
objects.paper_statement:
The minimax risk over the model class
\(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) at the evaluation dose \(t_0\) is
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
=
\inf_{\substack{\hat\theta_n\ \mathrm{measurable}\\
\hat\theta_n(O_1,\ldots,O_n)\in[-M,M]\ \mathrm{for\ every\ sample}}}
\sup_{P\in \mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)}
E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[
\left\{\hat\theta_n(O_1,\ldots,O_n)-\theta_P(t_0)\right\}^2
\right],
\]
where \(P^{\otimes n}\) is the product law of the iid sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\).

objects.title: Published HOIF benchmark $\rho_n$
objects.paper_statement:
The published higher-order influence-function benchmark rate is
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\vee
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)} .
\]

objects.title: Beta frontier handle $\mathfrak B_{\alpha,\beta,s}$
objects.paper_statement:
The beta comparison handle \(\mathfrak B_{\alpha,\beta,s}\) denotes the following frontier statement. For every \(\beta>0\), a two-point Le Cam construction perturbing only the treatment regression \(\mu_P\), with the covariate density and treatment-density baselines fixed, yields the all-\(\beta\) lower floor
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\ge
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)} .
\]
When \(s\ge d/4\), the published benchmark \(\rho_n\) has this same exponent, so the converse is on the classical interior treatment-regression scale. When \(0<s<d/4\),
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
is recorded only as a published upper-side comparator, not as a same-class certified upper endpoint. The remaining unrestricted upper-frontier question is whether \(\rho_n\) is attainable on the original class, or whether an intermediate or genuinely \(\beta\)-sensitive phase intervenes.

objects.title: Full beta frontier
objects.paper_statement:
Under the baseline-submodel slack condition, the original class
\(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) satisfies the all-\(\beta\) lower floor
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\ge
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\]
for all sufficiently large \(n\). When \(s\ge d/4\), this lower floor has the same exponent as \(\rho_n\). When \(0<s<d/4\),
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
has a strictly smaller exponent and remains only a published upper-side comparator. The open comparison question \(\mathfrak Q_{\alpha,\beta,s}\) is whether the original class admits an upper bound of order \(\rho_n\), an intermediate exponent, or a genuinely \(\beta\)-sensitive phase.

objects.title: Bernoulli mean-channel KL bound
objects.paper_statement:
Fix \(B>0\). Let \(Q_u\) and \(Q_v\) be distributions on \(\{-B,B\}\) with means \(u\) and \(v\), respectively, so that
\[
Q_u(B)=\frac{1+u/B}{2},
\qquad
Q_v(B)=\frac{1+v/B}{2}.
\]
If \(|u|\le B/2\) and \(|v|\le B/2\), then
\[
\mathrm{KL}(Q_u,Q_v)
\le
\frac{2(u-v)^2}{B^2}.
\]

objects.title: Le Cam Two-Point Bound
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(Q_0\) and \(Q_1\) be two laws for the \(n\)-sample experiment, and let \(\theta_0\) and \(\theta_1\) be the corresponding values of a real parameter. Set
\[
\Delta = |\theta_1-\theta_0|.
\]
If
\[
\mathrm{KL}(Q_0,Q_1)\le K<\infty,
\]
then there exists a constant \(c_K>0\), depending only on \(K\), such that every estimator \(T\) satisfies
\[
\max_{i=0,1} E_{Q_i}\!\left[(T-\theta_i)^2\right]\ge c_K \Delta^2 .
\]

objects.title: Smooth-Covariate Rate Collapse
objects.paper_statement:
Let $\rho_n$ be the published HOIF benchmark of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, evaluated at $(n,\alpha,s,d)$. Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive treatment smoothness.)} $\alpha>0$.
\item \textbf{(Positive covariate smoothness.)} $s>0$.
\item \textbf{(Smooth-covariate regime.)} $d\le 4s$.
\item \textbf{(Sample size.)} $n\ge 1$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)} .
\]

objects.title: Deficient Regime Algebra
Published HOIF Comparator
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{BK}}_{\alpha,\beta,s,s_p}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) --- the \emph{Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class} --- be the Hölder dose class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} intersected with two additional law-level regularity conditions of \citet[Theorem~3.1, \S3.4]{bonvini2022}: (i) an upper bound on the covariate marginal density on the unit cube, \(p_X(x)\le\overline M\) for all \(x\in[0,1]^d\), which together with the lower local-positivity floor \(c_0\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} gives two-sided density control near \(t_0\) (their Conditions~2 and~4); and (ii) higher covariate-density Hölder smoothness of an order \(s_p\ge s\) under which the third-order remainder \(\lVert v\rVert_g\lVert q\rVert_g\lVert f\rVert_\infty^{m-1}\) is negligible (their \S3.4 discussion), the decisive condition that Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} does \emph{not} impose. Bonvini--Kennedy's Condition~3, that the projection kernel satisfies \(\sup_x\Pi(x,x)\lesssim k\), constrains the estimator's basis rather than the data law, and so is not part of the class. By construction \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{BK}}_{\alpha,\beta,s,s_p}\subseteq\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}\). Writing \(\bar R_n^{\mathrm{BK}}\) for the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} taken over \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{BK}}_{\alpha,\beta,s,s_p}\), it is known from \citet[Theorem~3.1]{bonvini2022} that the published \(m\)th-order higher-order influence-function estimator attains the benchmark rate: there exists a constant \(C>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
\bar R_n^{\mathrm{BK}} \le C \rho_n .
\]
Because \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{BK}}_{\alpha,\beta,s,s_p}\subseteq\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}\), this bounds the minimax risk over the strictly smaller, more regular class only, and does not imply any upper bound over the same-class Hölder dose model \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

objects.title: Hölder Dose Class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\)
objects.paper_statement:
With the minimax risk \(R_n(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0)\) and the published HOIF benchmark \(\rho_n\) as in Definitions~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, define
\[
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)
\]
to be the collection of laws \(P\) satisfying all of the following conditions:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sampling.)} The observations satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling}.
\item \textbf{(Causal identification.)} Consistency and no unmeasured confounding hold as in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:consistency} and~\ref{obj:ass:no-unmeasured-confounding}.
\item \textbf{(Outcome boundedness.)} The bounded-outcome condition holds as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}.
\item \textbf{(Interior dose.)} The evaluation dose \(t_0\) and local radius \(\varepsilon_0\) satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:interior-dose}.
\item \textbf{(Local positivity.)} The treatment density obeys the local positivity condition with constant \(c_0\) as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:local-positivity}.
\item \textbf{(Treatment-direction smoothness.)} The maps \(a\mapsto \mu_P(a,x)\) and \(a\mapsto \pi_P(a\mid x)\) satisfy the treatment-direction Hölder conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:mu-treatment-holder} and~\ref{obj:ass:pi-treatment-holder}.
\item \textbf{(Covariate-direction smoothness.)} The relevant covariate functions for \(\mu_P\), \(\pi_P\), and \(p_{X,P}\) satisfy the Hölder conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:mu-covariate-holder}, \ref{obj:ass:pi-covariate-holder}, and~\ref{obj:ass:px-holder}.
\end{itemize}

objects.title: Sharp Pointwise Lower Bound
objects.paper_statement:
For every covariate dimension $d$ and every real constants
$\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0$, suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive smoothness.)} $\alpha>0$, $\beta>0$, and $s>0$.
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} $M>0$, $c_0>0$, $t_0\in(0,1)$, $\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)$, and the local window determined by $(t_0,\varepsilon_0)$ is contained in $(0,1)$.
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for $(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant $c>0$ such that, for all sufficiently large sample sizes $n$,
\[
  c\, n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
  \le
  R_n\!\left(
    \mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),
    t_0
  \right),
\]
where $\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$ is the model class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} and $R_n$ is the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

objects.title: Oracle Regime Reduction
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(d\) be the covariate dimension, and let \(\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\) be constants. Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Well-formed constants.)} \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), \(s>0\), \(M>0\), \(c_0>0\), \(t_0\in(0,1)\), \(\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)\), and the window \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) is contained in \((0,1)\).
\item \textbf{(Oracle regime.)} \(d\le 4s\).
\item \textbf{(Baseline slack.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for \(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large sample sizes \(n\),
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\]
for the published HOIF benchmark rate \(\rho_n\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, and
\[
c\,\rho_n
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right),
\]
where the model class is Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} and the minimax risk is Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

objects.title: All-Beta Oracle Floor
objects.paper_statement:
Fix a covariate dimension $d\in\mathbb N$ and constants
$\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R$. Suppose that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive smoothness.)} $\alpha>0$, $\beta>0$, and $s>0$.
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} $M>0$, $c_0>0$, $t_0\in(0,1)$, $\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)$, and the window of radius $\varepsilon_0$ around $t_0$ lies in the interior dose region.
\item \textbf{(Slack baseline.)} The strict-slack baseline condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for $(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant $c_{\mathrm{or}}>0$ such that, for all sufficiently large $n\in\mathbb N$,
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\ge
c_{\mathrm{or}}\, n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)} .
\]
Here $\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$ is the model class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}, and $R_n$ is the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

objects.title: Smooth Covariate Minimax Floor
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(d\in\mathbb N\) and let \(\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R\). Suppose that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Smoothness.)} \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), and \(s>0\).
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} \(M>0\), \(c_0>0\), \(t_0\in(0,1)\), \(\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)\), and the window \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) is contained in \((0,1)\).
\item \textbf{(Smooth-covariate regime.)} \(d\le 4s\).
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for \((d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0
\right),
\]
where \(R_n\) is the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} over the same-class model of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}. Moreover,
\[
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
=
\rho_n,
\]
with \(\rho_n\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}.

objects.title: Deficient Frontier Bracket
objects.paper_statement:
Fix a covariate dimension $d\in\mathbb N$ and constants
$\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R$.  Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)}  $\alpha>0$, $\beta>0$, $s>0$, $M>0$, $c_0>0$, $t_0\in(0,1)$, $\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)$, and the window $[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]$ lies in $(0,1)$.
\item \textbf{(Deficient covariate smoothness.)}  $4s<d$.
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)}  Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for $(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant $c>0$ such that, for all sufficiently large sample sizes $n$,
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0
\right),
\]
where the minimax risk is as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and the class is Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}.  Moreover, for the published HOIF benchmark of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate},
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-\,2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
and its exponent is strictly smaller than the certified lower-bound exponent:
\[
\frac{2}{1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha}
<
\frac{2\alpha}{2\alpha+1}.
\]

objects.title: Certified Beta Frontier Bracket
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(d\in\mathbb N\), and let \(\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), \(s>0\), \(M>0\), \(c_0>0\), \(t_0\in(0,1)\), \(\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)\), and the window \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) lies in \((0,1)\).
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for \(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right),
\]
where the minimax risk and the same-class Hölder dose-response model are those of Definitions~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}. Moreover, for every \(n\ge 1\), the benchmark \(\rho_n\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate} satisfies
\[
d\le 4s
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\rho_n=n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)},
\]
and
\[
4s<d
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\rho_n=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\quad\text{and}\quad
\frac{2}{1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha}
<
\frac{2\alpha}{2\alpha+1}.
\]

f2d06fe61dcd28d9.label: IidSampling
f2d06fe61dcd28d9.statement:
/-- `O_1, …, O_n` are i.i.d. draws from `P`. Carries the full i.i.d. content by
reusing `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample` (mutually independent, identically distributed
measurable maps with law `P.dataMeasure`), together with
`IsProbabilityMeasure P.dataMeasure` certifying that the per-draw law — hence the
`n`-fold product `Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P.dataMeasure)` formed in
`minimaxRisk` — is a probability measure. Also pins the observed-unit support
`O = (Y,A,X)` to the sampling space `{(Y,A,X): Y∈ℝ, A∈[0,1], X∈[0,1]^d}`: under `P`
the treatment `A ∈ [0,1]` and the covariate vector `X ∈ [0,1]^d` almost surely. -/
def IidSampling {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop :=
  IsProbabilityMeasure P.dataMeasure ∧
    (∀ᵐ O ∂P.dataMeasure, O.A ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) ∧
      -- @realizes A(a.s. treatment range A ∈ [0,1] under P — THE enforcing predicate pinning the carrier `DoseObs.A : ℝ` to its declared space [0,1]; jointly with the X conjunct below it also pins the treatment coordinate of the observed unit O's a.s. joint support {(Y,A,X): Y∈ℝ, A∈[0,1], X∈[0,1]^d})
    (∀ᵐ O ∂P.dataMeasure, O.X ∈ cube d) ∧
      -- @realizes X(a.s. covariate range X ∈ [0,1]^d under P — THE enforcing predicate pinning the carrier `DoseObs.X : Fin d → ℝ` to its declared cube [0,1]^d; jointly with the A conjunct above it also pins the covariate coordinate of O's a.s. joint support)
    -- @realizes O_1, ..., O_n(i.i.d. n-sample: mutually independent, identically `P.dataMeasure`-distributed measurable draws via `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample` — THIS `IidSampling` i.i.d. realizer is one of the two load-bearing realizers of the sample symbol `O_1,…,O_n` (the other is the `minimaxRisk` `Measure.pi` product law). It is NOT realized by the FOLLOWING `Consistency` declaration; the tag is placed BEFORE the existential so it attaches to `IidSampling`, not to the next decl. Each draw is a.s. in `{A∈[0,1], X∈[0,1]^d}`, and the n-fold product `Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P.dataMeasure)` formed in `minimaxRisk` is their joint law.)
    ∃ (Ω : Type) (_mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω) (μ : @MeasureTheory.Measure Ω _mΩ),
      Nonempty (@Causalean.Stat.IIDSample Ω (DoseObs d) _mΩ
        instMeasurableSpaceDoseObs μ P.dataMeasure)

311fc9a1432e76ee.label: Consistency
311fc9a1432e76ee.statement:
/-- Consistency `Y = Y(A)` almost surely under `P`: the realized outcome equals the
potential outcome at the realized treatment level. -/
def Consistency {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop :=
  ∀ᵐ O ∂P.dataMeasure, O.Y = P.pot O.A O

e4760f69a703f99f.label: NoUnmeasuredConfounding
e4760f69a703f99f.statement:
/-- Conditional ignorability for the continuous treatment: for every level
`a ∈ [0,1]`, the potential outcome `Y(a)` is independent of `A` given `X` under `P`
(the note restricts the dose to the treatment range `[0,1]`). Encoded in
the faithful tested form `E[f(Y(a)) ∣ A, X] = E[f(Y(a)) ∣ X]` for every bounded
measurable test `f` of the potential outcome (the standard conditional-independence
characterization, no `StandardBorelSpace` requirement). -/
def NoUnmeasuredConfounding {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop :=
  ∀ a ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, ∀ (f : ℝ → ℝ), Measurable f → (∃ Mf : ℝ, ∀ y, |f y| ≤ Mf) →
    P.dataMeasure[(fun O => f (P.pot a O)) |
        MeasurableSpace.comap (fun O : DoseObs d => (O.A, O.X)) inferInstance]
      =ᵐ[P.dataMeasure]
    P.dataMeasure[(fun O => f (P.pot a O)) |
        MeasurableSpace.comap (fun O : DoseObs d => O.X) inferInstance]

4b55d3f108810a95.label: BoundedOutcome

2d49cfcaa390b23f.label: InteriorDose

780214bc34555f81.label: LocalPositivity
780214bc34555f81.statement:
/-- Local positivity: `π_P(a∣x) ≥ c_0` for every `a` in the `ε_0`-window and every
`x ∈ [0,1]^d`. -/
def LocalPositivity {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (c0 t0 eps0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ a ∈ doseWindow t0 eps0, ∀ x ∈ cube d, c0 ≤ P.pi a x

6f151e55a7a85eea.label: MuTreatmentHolder
6f151e55a7a85eea.statement:
/-- Treatment-direction smoothness: for every `x ∈ [0,1]^d`, the slice
`a ↦ μ_P(a,x)` lies in a Hölder ball of order `α`, radius `M`, on the window. -/
def MuTreatmentHolder {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (alpha M t0 eps0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ cube d, HolderBall1D (fun a => P.mu a x) alpha M (doseWindow t0 eps0)

821b92e89fcf2f60.label: PiTreatmentHolder
821b92e89fcf2f60.statement:
/-- Treatment-direction smoothness of the conditional treatment density: for every
`x ∈ [0,1]^d`, `a ↦ π_P(a∣x)` lies in a Hölder ball of order `β`, radius `M`, on
the window. -/
def PiTreatmentHolder {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (beta M t0 eps0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ cube d, HolderBall1D (fun a => P.pi a x) beta M (doseWindow t0 eps0)

815372f96fc57700.label: MuCovariateHolder
815372f96fc57700.statement:
/-- Covariate-direction smoothness: `x ↦ μ_P(t_0,x)` lies in a Hölder ball of
order `s`, radius `M`, on `[0,1]^d`. -/
def MuCovariateHolder {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (s M t0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  HolderBallND (fun x => P.mu t0 x) s M (cube d)

0557aa7a7c1be8b5.label: PiCovariateHolder
0557aa7a7c1be8b5.statement:
/-- Covariate-direction smoothness of the conditional treatment density:
`x ↦ π_P(t_0∣x)` lies in a Hölder ball of order `s`, radius `M`, on `[0,1]^d`. -/
def PiCovariateHolder {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (s M t0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  HolderBallND (fun x => P.pi t0 x) s M (cube d)

db2ea5e88dad72d8.label: PxHolder
db2ea5e88dad72d8.statement:
/-- Covariate-density smoothness and range: `p_{X,P}` lies in a Hölder ball of order
`s`, radius `M`, on `[0,1]^d`, is NONNEGATIVE (`0 ≤ p_{X,P}` on the cube — the declared
density range `[0,∞)`), and is bounded above by `M`. The nonnegativity conjunct makes
`p_{X,P}` a genuine density object: with it `ENNReal.ofReal (P.px x) = P.px x` on the
cube, so the `PxIsXDensity` tie realizes the ACTUAL `p_{X,P}` density rather than only
its `ofReal` positive part. -/
def PxHolder {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (s M : ℝ) : Prop :=
  HolderBallND P.px s M (cube d) ∧
    (∀ x ∈ cube d, 0 ≤ P.px x) ∧     -- @realizes p_{X,P}(0 ≤ p_{X,P} on cube; enforces the declared density range [0,∞), so the `ofReal` `PxIsXDensity` tie realizes the actual nonnegative density object)
    (∀ x ∈ cube d, P.px x ≤ M)

e37514c6ac6327f7.label: BaselineSubmodelSlack
e37514c6ac6327f7.statement:
/-- Nonempty-interior-of-model (strict-slack baseline) existence hypothesis: there
exist a covariate density `p_0` on `[0,1]^d`, a conditional treatment density `q_0`
on `[0,1]`, a slack `η_0 > 0`, and an outcome scale `B_0 ∈ (0,M)`, such that `p_0`
and `q_0` have Hölder norms at most `M - η_0` in the classes required of `p_{X,P}`
and `π_P`, `q_0(a) ≥ c_0 + η_0` on the `ε_0`-window, `p_0 ≤ M - η_0`, and the
constant-zero outcome regression admits a symmetric two-point channel on
`{-B_0, B_0} ⊆ [-M,M]`. Provisional D0.R refinement (`user_approved = false`). -/
def BaselineSubmodelSlack (d : ℕ) (beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (p0 : (Fin d → ℝ) → ℝ) (q0 : ℝ → ℝ) (eta0 B0 : ℝ),
    0 < eta0 ∧ 0 < B0 ∧ B0 < M ∧
    (∀ x ∈ cube d, 0 ≤ p0 x) ∧ (∀ a, 0 ≤ q0 a) ∧
    (∫ x in cube d, p0 x) = 1 ∧ (∫ a in Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, q0 a) = 1 ∧
    HolderBallND p0 s (M - eta0) (cube d) ∧ (∀ x ∈ cube d, p0 x ≤ M - eta0) ∧
    HolderBall1D q0 beta (M - eta0) (doseWindow t0 eps0) ∧
    (∀ a ∈ doseWindow t0 eps0, c0 + eta0 ≤ q0 a)

cdd093b50946d727.label: thetaFunctional
cdd093b50946d727.statement:
/-- Identifying partial-mean (backdoor-adjustment) functional
`θ_P(t_0) = ∫_{[0,1]^d} μ_P(t_0,x) p_{X,P}(x) dx` (Lebesgue). Under consistency and
ignorability this equals the causal dose-response value `E_P[Y(t_0)]`. -/
noncomputable def thetaFunctional {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) (t0 : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  ∫ x in cube d, P.mu t0 x * P.px x

99eed3aca2f726f2.label: minimaxRisk
99eed3aca2f726f2.statement:
/-- Pointwise minimax mean-squared risk
`R_n(C, t_0) = inf_{θ̂_n} sup_{P ∈ C} E_P[(θ̂_n - θ_P(t_0))^2]`.
The infimum ranges over MEASURABLE estimators truncated to `[-M,M]` (the
bounded-estimand range: truncating to the interval containing every `θ_P(t_0)`
never increases MSE and yields the SAME minimax value, while keeping the inner
squared loss bounded by `(2M)^2` under each `n`-fold probability law — so the
`⨆`/`⨅` are well-posed, with no junk-`0` inflation of the converse). -/
noncomputable def minimaxRisk {d : ℕ} (M : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
    (C : DoseLaw d → Prop) (t0 : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  ⨅ est : {est : (Fin n → DoseObs d) → ℝ //
      Measurable est ∧ ∀ s, est s ∈ Set.Icc (-M) M},
      -- @realizes O_1, ..., O_n(estimator domain is the size-`n` sample `Fin n → DoseObs d`)
    ⨆ P : {P : DoseLaw d // C P},
      -- @realizes O_1, ..., O_n(the i.i.d. `n`-sample as the `n`-fold PRODUCT LAW `Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P.dataMeasure)` over `Fin n → DoseObs d` — THIS `minimaxRisk` product law is the second of the two load-bearing realizers of the sample symbol (the first is the `IidSampling` i.i.d. realizer); its factors are mutually independent and identically `P.dataMeasure`-distributed exactly by the `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample` conjunct of `IidSampling`. It is NOT realized by the FOLLOWING `publishedHoifRate` declaration; the tag is placed BEFORE the integral so it attaches to `minimaxRisk`, not to the next decl.)
      ∫ s, (est.1 s - thetaFunctional P.1 t0) ^ 2
        ∂(Measure.pi fun _ : Fin n => (P.1).dataMeasure)

114ae971357cc657.label: publishedHoifRate
114ae971357cc657.statement:
/-- Published Bonvini–Kennedy benchmark rate
`ρ_n = n^{-2α/(2α+1)} ∨ n^{-2/(1 + d/(4s) + 1/α)}`.
@realizes rho_n(the frontier sequence `n ↦ ρ_n`; this `def` is the authoritative realizer of the setup symbol `ρ_n`, and its declared range `[0,∞)` is ENFORCED by `publishedHoifRate_nonneg` below) -/
noncomputable def publishedHoifRate (n : ℕ) (alpha s : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  max ((n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1))))
      ((n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha))))

f3de0cf51d71a2b5.label: betaFrontierHandle
f3de0cf51d71a2b5.statement:
/-- Headline frontier handle. Records the DELIVERED content: the all-β oracle lower
floor `R_n ≥ c n^{-2α/(2α+1)}` on the original class, together with the
regime-by-regime `ρ_n` comparison — `ρ_n` collapses to the oracle exponent when
`s ≥ d/4` (`d ≤ 4s`), and has a strictly smaller exponent when `0 < s < d/4`
(`4s < d`). Stated as a `Prop`, NOT asserted as a same-class upper endpoint. -/
def betaFrontierHandle {d : ℕ} (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  (∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
      c * (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
        ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0)
    ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, (d : ℝ) ≤ 4 * s →
        publishedHoifRate n alpha s d = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1))))
    -- deficient regime `0 < s < d/4`: BOTH the `ρ_n` branch IDENTITY and the strict
    -- exponent comparison to the oracle floor (note `lem:rho-deficient-regime-algebra`).
    ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 4 * s < (d : ℝ) →
        publishedHoifRate n alpha s d
          = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha))))
    ∧ (4 * s < (d : ℝ) →
        2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha) < 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1))

851ba863ab8e06d4.label: fullBetaFrontierResidual
851ba863ab8e06d4.statement:
def fullBetaFrontierResidual {d : ℕ} (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ) : Prop :=
  BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0 →
    betaFrontierHandle (d := d) alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 ∧
      -- THE open question, as the note's three-way disjunction of unresolved
      -- alternatives. Never proved; stating it asserts no branch.
      ( -- (i) `ρ_n` attainable: a same-class upper bound of order `ρ_n`.
        (∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
          minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0
            ≤ C * publishedHoifRate n alpha s d)
        ∨ -- (ii) an INTERMEDIATE exponent strictly between the deficient `ρ_n`
          --      exponent and the oracle floor exponent.
        (∃ kappa C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧
          2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha) < kappa ∧
          kappa < 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1) ∧
          ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
            minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0
              ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ (-kappa))
        ∨ -- (iii) a genuinely β-SENSITIVE phase: distinct `β₁ ≠ β₂` yield two-sided
          --       minimax rates of different exponents `κ₁ ≠ κ₂`.
        (∃ beta1 beta2 kappa1 kappa2 c1 C1 c2 C2 : ℝ,
          beta1 ≠ beta2 ∧ 0 < beta1 ∧ 0 < beta2 ∧ kappa1 ≠ kappa2 ∧
          0 < c1 ∧ 0 < C1 ∧ 0 < c2 ∧ 0 < C2 ∧
          (∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
            c1 * (n : ℝ) ^ (-kappa1)
                ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta1 s M c0 eps0 t0) t0 ∧
            minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta1 s M c0 eps0 t0) t0
                ≤ C1 * (n : ℝ) ^ (-kappa1)) ∧
          (∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
            c2 * (n : ℝ) ^ (-kappa2)
                ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta2 s M c0 eps0 t0) t0 ∧
            minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta2 s M c0 eps0 t0) t0
                ≤ C2 * (n : ℝ) ^ (-kappa2))) )

dbb9e5efdfcc64d4.label: rho_oracle_regime_algebra
dbb9e5efdfcc64d4.statement:
/-- Smooth-covariate collapse: if `0 < α`, `0 < s`, and `d ≤ 4 s` (i.e. `s ≥ d/4`),
then `ρ_n = n^{-2α/(2α+1)}` for every `n ≥ 1`. -/
lemma rho_oracle_regime_algebra (n : ℕ) (alpha s : ℝ) (d : ℕ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hs : 0 < s) (hsd : (d : ℝ) ≤ 4 * s) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    publishedHoifRate n alpha s d = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))

897b0dcc814ca922.label: rho_deficient_regime_algebra
897b0dcc814ca922.statement:
/-- Deficient-covariate collapse: if `0 < α`, `0 < s`, and `4 s < d` (i.e.
`0 < s < d/4`), then `ρ_n = n^{-2/(1 + d/(4s) + 1/α)}` for every `n ≥ 1`, and the
deficient exponent is strictly smaller than the oracle exponent. -/
lemma rho_deficient_regime_algebra (n : ℕ) (alpha s : ℝ) (d : ℕ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hs : 0 < s) (hsd : 4 * s < (d : ℝ)) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    publishedHoifRate n alpha s d
        = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha)))
      ∧ 2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha) < 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1) := by
  have hs4 : 0 < 4 * s := by positivity
  have hone_lt : 1 < (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) := by
    rw [one_lt_div hs4]
    simpa using hsd
  have hden_pos : 0 < 2 + 1 / alpha := by positivity
  have hden_lt : 2 + 1 / alpha < 1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha := by
    linarith
  have hfrac :
      2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha) < 2 / (2 + 1 / alpha) := by
    exact div_lt_div_of_pos_left (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) < 2) hden_pos hden_lt
  have hid : 2 / (2 + 1 / alpha) = 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1) := by
    field_simp [halpha.ne']
  have hexp : 2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha)
      < 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1) := by
    rwa [hid] at hfrac
  constructor
  · by_cases hn1 : n = 1
    · subst n
      simp [publishedHoifRate]
    · have hnat : 1 < n := by omega
      have hbase : (1 : ℝ) < (n : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast hnat
      have hpow : (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
          ≤ (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha))) := by
        rw [Real.rpow_le_rpow_left_iff hbase]
        linarith
      rw [publishedHoifRate]
      exact max_eq_right hpow
  · exact hexp

end CausalSmith.Stat.DoseResponseMinimax

8b9ac7bc08f3aac8.label: publishedUpperBoundCited
8b9ac7bc08f3aac8.statement:
/-- **Cited (Bonvini–Kennedy 2022, Theorem 3.1, `arXiv:2207.11825`).** The published
higher-order influence-function estimator attains the benchmark rate over the
Bonvini–Kennedy localized-regularity class: there is `C > 0` such that the minimax MSE
over `bkLocalizedDoseClass` is at most `C · publishedHoifRate n α s d` for all large
`n`. This is a CITED comparator that holds on Bonvini–Kennedy's stricter localized
class — **not** on the bare Hölder class `HolderDoseClass`, where the matching
same-class upper endpoint remains OPEN (see `oeq:full-beta-frontier`). Since
`bkLocalizedDoseClass ⊆ HolderDoseClass`, this upper bound does not contradict the
certified same-class lower bound. It is kept purely as a supportive comparator and is
assumed by no certified result. -/
def publishedUpperBoundCited (d : ℕ) (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 sp : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ C, 0 < C ∧ ∀ᶠ (n : ℕ) in atTop,
    minimaxRisk M n (bkLocalizedDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 sp) t0
      ≤ C * publishedHoifRate n alpha s d

598d16d901a80b42.label: HolderDoseClass
598d16d901a80b42.statement:
/-- The anisotropic Hölder dose-response model class
`P_{α,β,s}(M,c_0,ε_0,t_0)`: the bundle of the member-atom properties, including the three
SEMANTIC TIES (`μ_P` is the data regression, `p_{X,P}` is the X-marginal density, and
`π_P` is the law's conditional treatment density) that make it a genuine CAUSAL class and
pin it to EXACTLY the paper class (so `minimaxRisk` over it is the true causal minimax
risk and `thetaFunctional` is `E_P[Y(t_0)]`, not a free-field artifact, and the class is
not strictly broader than `P_{α,β,s}` through a free `π` field).
(`ass:baseline-submodel-slack` is NOT a member; it is a separate theorem hypothesis.)
The two regime theorems take `(hP : HolderDoseClass … P)`. -/
structure HolderDoseClass (d : ℕ) (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop where
  iid : IidSampling P
  consistency : Consistency P
  ignorability : NoUnmeasuredConfounding P
  bdd : BoundedOutcome P M
  interior : InteriorDose t0 eps0
  positivity : LocalPositivity P c0 t0 eps0
  muT : MuTreatmentHolder P alpha M t0 eps0
  piT : PiTreatmentHolder P beta M t0 eps0
  muX : MuCovariateHolder P s M t0
  piX : PiCovariateHolder P s M t0
  pxH : PxHolder P s M
  muReg : MuIsRegression P
  pxDens : PxIsXDensity P
  piCond : PiIsCondTreatmentDensity P

cc720918cf07f6f7.label: MuIsRegression
cc720918cf07f6f7.statement:
/-- **Semantic tie (`μ` is the data regression).** The conditional mean of `Y` given
`(A,X)` under the data law equals `μ_P(A,X)`. This pins the free `mu` field to the
actual law, so `thetaFunctional = ∫ μ_P(t_0,·)·p_{X,P}` is the GENUINE causal value
`E_P[Y(t_0)]` (under consistency + ignorability). Without it `mu` is disconnected from
`dataMeasure`, which permits a vacuous two-point construction (a Dirac data law with
the separation carried only by the free `mu`); enforcing it makes `minimaxRisk` the
true causal minimax risk. -/
def MuIsRegression {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop :=
  P.dataMeasure[(fun O => O.Y) |
      MeasurableSpace.comap (fun O : DoseObs d => (O.A, O.X)) inferInstance]
    =ᵐ[P.dataMeasure] (fun O => P.mu O.A O.X)

ef2d13fdac8af3aa.label: PxIsXDensity
ef2d13fdac8af3aa.statement:
/-- **Semantic tie (`p_{X,P}` is the X-marginal density).** The pushforward of the
data law under `X` is `p_{X,P}·(Lebesgue ↾ [0,1]^d)`, tying the free `px` field to the
actual covariate law so `thetaFunctional` integrates `μ` against the genuine `P_X`. -/
def PxIsXDensity {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop :=
  P.dataMeasure.map (fun O => O.X)
    = (volume.restrict (cube d)).withDensity (fun x => ENNReal.ofReal (P.px x))

280e450600b04842.label: PiIsCondTreatmentDensity
280e450600b04842.statement:
/-- **Semantic tie / range-normalization (`π_P` is the conditional treatment density).**
The pushforward of the data law under `(A,X)` equals the measure with joint density
`(a,x) ↦ π_P(a∣x)·p_{X,P}(x)` w.r.t. Lebesgue on `[0,1]×[0,1]^d`. Together with
`PxIsXDensity` (the `X`-marginal is `p_{X,P}·Leb`) this pins `π_P(·∣x)` to be the GENUINE
conditional density of `A` given `X = x` under `P` — it is normalized
(`∫_{[0,1]} π_P(a∣x) da = 1` for `P_X`-a.e. `x`, forced by consistency of the joint with
its `X`-marginal) and tied to `dataMeasure`. Without it the `pi` field is disconnected
from `dataMeasure`, so the class `P_{α,β,s}` would be strictly BROADER than the paper
class (an arbitrary `π` field satisfying only the Hölder/positivity atoms, never required
to be the law's actual treatment density). The core symbol `pi_P(a∣x)` is *defined* as the
conditional density of `A` given `X = x` under `P`, so this tie is exactly the note's
stated meaning of `π_P`, not an added restriction. The leading conjunct ENFORCES the
declared density range `0 ≤ π_P` on `[0,1]×[0,1]^d`: with it `ENNReal.ofReal (π_P·p_{X,P})`
genuinely equals `π_P·p_{X,P}` (both factors nonnegative, using `PxHolder`'s `0 ≤ p_{X,P}`),
so the tie realizes the ACTUAL nonnegative conditional treatment density object, not only
its `ofReal` positive part.
@realizes pi_P(`0 ≤ π_P` on `[0,1]×cube` enforcing the declared range `[0,∞)`, AND `P.dataMeasure.map (A,X) = (Leb↾[0,1] ⊗ Leb↾[0,1]^d).withDensity (π_P·p_{X,P})`; together the range-clause + joint-factorization tie pin `pi` to the law's genuine nonnegative conditional treatment density) -/
def PiIsCondTreatmentDensity {d : ℕ} (P : DoseLaw d) : Prop :=
  (∀ a ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, ∀ x ∈ cube d, 0 ≤ P.pi a x) ∧   -- @realizes pi_P(0 ≤ pi_P on [0,1]×cube; enforces the declared density range [0,∞), so the `ofReal` joint-density tie realizes the actual nonnegative conditional treatment density)
    P.dataMeasure.map (fun O => (O.A, O.X))
      = ((volume.restrict (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)).prod (volume.restrict (cube d))).withDensity
          (fun p => ENNReal.ofReal (P.pi p.1 p.2 * P.px p.2))

777734e5696f1a60.label: sharp_pointwise_lower_bound
777734e5696f1a60.statement:
/-- Certified kernel converse. Assume the strict-slack baseline. For every `β > 0`
there is `c > 0`, depending only on the fixed model radii and the slack baseline,
such that for all sufficiently large `n`,
`R_n(P_{α,β,s}(M,c_0,ε_0,t_0), t_0) ≥ c n^{-2α/(2α+1)}` — the classical interior
treatment-regression pointwise rate, uniformly over every `β > 0`. -/
theorem sharp_pointwise_lower_bound {d : ℕ}
    (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hbeta : 0 < beta) (hs : 0 < s)
    (hreg : RegimeConstants alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0)
    (hslack : BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0) :
    ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
      c * (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
        ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0

21dd8517e2cf3321.label: oracle_regime_reduction
21dd8517e2cf3321.statement:
/-- Oracle regime reduction. Under the assumptions of
`sharp_pointwise_lower_bound`, if `s ≥ d/4` (`d ≤ 4s`) then `ρ_n = n^{-2α/(2α+1)}`
and hence `R_n ≥ c n^{-2α/(2α+1)} = c ρ_n` for some `c > 0`. The certified lower
floor reduces to the classical interior pointwise nonparametric regression
barrier. -/
theorem oracle_regime_reduction {d : ℕ}
    (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hbeta : 0 < beta) (hs : 0 < s)
    (hreg : RegimeConstants alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0)
    (hsd : (d : ℝ) ≤ 4 * s)
    (hslack : BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0) :
    ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
      publishedHoifRate n alpha s d = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
        ∧ c * publishedHoifRate n alpha s d
            ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0

5a3c6ec9f6925185.label: oracle_dose_regression_lower_all_beta
5a3c6ec9f6925185.statement:
/-- The all-β oracle lower floor. Assume the strict-slack baseline. For every
`β > 0` there is `c_or > 0`, depending only on the fixed model radii and the slack
baseline (not on `n`), such that for all sufficiently large `n`,
`R_n(P_{α,β,s}(M,c_0,ε_0,t_0), t_0) ≥ c_or n^{-2α/(2α+1)}`. The construction
perturbs only the treatment regression and leaves the treatment density fixed, so
the bound is independent of whether `β ≥ α`. -/
lemma oracle_dose_regression_lower_all_beta {d : ℕ}
    (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hbeta : 0 < beta) (hs : 0 < s)
    (hreg : RegimeConstants alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0)
    (hslack : BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0) :
    ∃ cor : ℝ, 0 < cor ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
      cor * (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
        ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0

d91e4a69a2afa794.label: sharp_minimax_smooth_covariate
d91e4a69a2afa794.statement:
/-- Certified smooth-covariate regime theorem. Assume the strict-slack baseline.
For every `β > 0` and every `s ≥ d/4` (`d ≤ 4s`) there is `c > 0` such that, for
all sufficiently large `n`, `R_n ≥ c n^{-2α/(2α+1)} = c ρ_n`. The certified lower
floor lands on the same exponent as `ρ_n`; NO same-class upper bound is claimed. -/
theorem sharp_minimax_smooth_covariate {d : ℕ}
    (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hbeta : 0 < beta) (hs : 0 < s)
    (hreg : RegimeConstants alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0)
    (hsd : (d : ℝ) ≤ 4 * s)
    (hslack : BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0) :
    ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
      c * (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
          ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0
        ∧ (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1))) = publishedHoifRate n alpha s d

2122c101fe64ef68.label: frontier_bracket_deficient
2122c101fe64ef68.statement:
/-- Certified deficient-covariate regime theorem. Assume the strict-slack baseline.
For every `β > 0` and every `0 < s < d/4` (`4s < d`) there is `c > 0` such that,
for all sufficiently large `n`, `R_n ≥ c n^{-2α/(2α+1)}`, while
`ρ_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/α)}` has a strictly smaller exponent. The same-class upper
endpoint `C ρ_n` is NOT discharged. -/
theorem frontier_bracket_deficient {d : ℕ}
    (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hbeta : 0 < beta) (hs : 0 < s)
    (hreg : RegimeConstants alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0)
    (hsd : 4 * s < (d : ℝ))
    (hslack : BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0) :
    ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
      c * (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
          ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0
        ∧ publishedHoifRate n alpha s d
            = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha)))
        ∧ 2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha) < 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)

a1abac3294575f4f.label: certified_beta_frontier_bracket
a1abac3294575f4f.statement:
/-- Certified β-frontier bracket. Assume the strict-slack baseline. For every
`β > 0` there is `c > 0` with `R_n ≥ c n^{-2α/(2α+1)}` eventually; and — for EVERY
`n ≥ 1`, not merely eventually, matching the note's unconditional `ρ_n` algebra —
in the smooth-covariate regime `s ≥ d/4` (`d ≤ 4s`) the benchmark `ρ_n` collapses
to that oracle exponent, while in the deficient regime `0 < s < d/4` (`4s < d`)
`ρ_n` has a strictly smaller exponent. The regime identities are stated outside the
`∀ᶠ n` (eventually) clause so they are NOT weakened to large `n` only. No same-class
upper endpoint is packaged. -/
lemma certified_beta_frontier_bracket {d : ℕ}
    (alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0 : ℝ)
    (halpha : 0 < alpha) (hbeta : 0 < beta) (hs : 0 < s)
    (hreg : RegimeConstants alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0)
    (hslack : BaselineSubmodelSlack d beta s M c0 eps0 t0) :
    ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧
      (∀ᶠ n : ℕ in Filter.atTop,
        c * (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
          ≤ minimaxRisk M n (HolderDoseClass d alpha beta s M c0 eps0 t0) t0)
      ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 1 ≤ n →
          ((d : ℝ) ≤ 4 * s →
              publishedHoifRate n alpha s d = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1))))
          ∧ (4 * s < (d : ℝ) →
              publishedHoifRate n alpha s d
                  = (n : ℝ) ^ (-(2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha)))
                ∧ 2 / (1 + (d : ℝ) / (4 * s) + 1 / alpha) < 2 * alpha / (2 * alpha + 1)))
